bispecific antibodies.news: In April 2026, China's National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) granted conditional approval to IMDELLTRA (tarlatamab), a DLL3-targeting bispecific T-cell engager (BiTE), for third-line extensive-stage small cell lung cancer.

Amgen reported positive topline results from a Phase 3 trial of BLINCYTO (blinatumomab), a CD19-targeting bispecific T-cell engager, in combination with low-intensity chemotherapy for older adults with newly diagnosed B-cell precursor acute lymphoblastic leukemia.

Amgen initiated multiple Phase 3 and Phase 1b clinical studies for xaluritamig (AMG 509), a first-in-class bispecific T-cell engager targeting STEAP1, for the treatment of metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer and Ewing sarcoma.
Merck initiated a pivotal Phase 2b/3 clinical trial evaluating MK-8748, an investigational bispecific Tie2 agonist/VEGF inhibitor, for the treatment of neovascular age-related macular degeneration.

monoclonal antibodies.news: The FDA proposed withdrawing approval of TAVNEOS (avacopan) due to lack of substantial evidence of effectiveness and hepatotoxicity concerns, though the drug remains on the market pending a potential hearing.

The European Commission approved UPLIZNA (inebilizumab) as an add-on therapy for generalized myasthenia gravis in adult patients who are anti-acetylcholine receptor or anti-muscle specific tyrosine kinase antibody positive.
Amgen is advancing daxdilimab, a first-in-class plasmacytoid dendritic cell depleting monoclonal antibody, to a registrational phase of development.
A Phase 2 study of AMG 732, an insulin-like growth factor-1 receptor targeting monoclonal antibody, is currently enrolling patients with moderate-to-severe active thyroid eye disease.
The FDA approved KEYTRUDA (pembrolizumab) and KEYTRUDA QLEX plus paclitaxel, with or without bevacizumab, for the treatment of certain adults with PD-L1+ platinum-resistant ovarian cancer.
The European Commission approved a KEYTRUDA regimen plus paclitaxel, with or without bevacizumab, for the treatment of adults with PD-L1+ platinum-resistant recurrent ovarian carcinoma.
The FDA granted priority review for a label update for KEYTRUDA QLEX based on Phase 2 trial results evaluating patient preference for subcutaneous administration over intravenous administration.

The FDA granted priority review for KEYTRUDA and KEYTRUDA QLEX, each with Padcev, for cisplatin-eligible patients with muscle-invasive bladder cancer based on positive Phase 3 KEYNOTE-B15 trial results.

The FDA accepted supplemental applications for WELIREG in combination with KEYTRUDA or KEYTRUDA QLEX for the adjuvant treatment of certain patients with renal cell carcinoma, setting a PDUFA date of June 19, 2026.
The FDA accepted supplemental applications for WELIREG plus Lenvima in certain previously treated patients with advanced renal cell carcinoma, setting a PDUFA date of October 4, 2026.

The European Medicines Agency's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human View adopted a positive opinion for an expanded indication for CAPVAXIVE for active immunization against invasive pneumococcal disease in certain children and adolescents.

The FDA Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee (ODAC) voted 7 to 1 to recommend approval of Truqap (capivasertib) in combination with abiraterone and androgen deprivation therapy for PTEN-deficient metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer, based on positive Phase III CAPItello-281 trial results.

bispecific antibodies.news: Genentech announced it will present extensive data at ARVO 2026 showcasing its ophthalmology portfolio, including real-world data for Vabysmo (faricimab), the first bispecific antibody approved for the eye targeting Ang-2 and VEGF-A.

Pfizer announced positive topline results from the Phase 3 MagnetisMM-5 trial for ELREXFIO (elranatamab), a BCMA-CD3 directed bispecific antibody, demonstrating a statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival for patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma.

Roche announced it will present extensive real-world and clinical data for Vabysmo (faricimab), an industry-leading bispecific antibody targeting Ang-2 and VEGF-A, at the ARVO 2026 annual meeting. The data includes findings from the VOYAGER study demonstrating retinal drying in nAMD and DME, as well as long-term effectiveness results.

Pfizer announced positive topline results from the Phase 3 MagnetisMM-5 trial for ELREXFIO (elranatamab), a BCMAxCD3 bispecific antibody, showing statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival for relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma patients compared to standard of care.

Pfizer announced positive topline results from the Phase 3 MagnetisMM-5 trial for ELREXFIO (elranatamab), a BCMA-CD3 bispecific antibody. The study met its primary endpoint, showing a statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival for patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma compared to standard-of-care therapy.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Genentech announced it will present extensive data at ARVO 2026 showcasing its ophthalmology portfolio, including new real-world data for Vabysmo and early results for the investigational monoclonal antibody vamikibart in treating uveitic macular edema.

Pfizer announced positive topline results from the Phase 3 MagnetisMM-5 trial for ELREXFIO (elranatamab), demonstrating a statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival for patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma compared to standard-of-care therapy.
Roche presented first results from the phase 2 ALLUVIUM and BARDENAS trials for vamikibart, an investigational intravitreal monoclonal antibody targeting IL-6 for uveitic macular edema, at ARVO 2026.

AbbVie submitted a New Drug Application to the FDA for Skyrizi (risankizumab), a monoclonal antibody, for subcutaneous induction in moderately to severely active Crohn's disease, supported by Phase 3 AFFIRM study data.
AbbVie submitted a New Drug Application to the FDA for Rinvoq (upadacitinib) for a new indication in severe alopecia areata, supported by Phase 3 UP-AA clinical program data.

bispecific antibodies.news: Galapagos and Gilead entered into a binding agreement to collaborate on advancing a first-in-class T-cell engager program for autoimmune diseases. The company also announced the name change to Lakefront Biotherapeutics effective May 8, 2026.

monoclonal antibodies.news: GSK announced new product approvals for Exdensur in the EU and China for severe asthma with an eosinophilic phenotype and nasal polyps, and for Nucala in the EU for COPD. Both Exdensur and Nucala are monoclonal antibodies targeting IL-5 pathways.

gene editing.news: Affinia Therapeutics announced it will present preclinical data on AFTX-201, an AAV gene therapy for BAG3-associated dilated cardiomyopathy, at the ASGCT 2026 Annual Meeting. The company also highlighted its proprietary capsid engineering and manufacturing data, alongside a poster on an epigenetic-editing modality for Myotonic Dystrophy Type 1 developed with Modalis Therapeutics.

Affinia Therapeutics will present preclinical data on AFTX-201 for BAG3-associated dilated cardiomyopathy and highlight a collaboration with Modalis Therapeutics on an epigenetic-editing modality for Myotonic Dystrophy Type 1 at the ASGCT 2026 Annual Meeting.

monoclonal antibodies.news: The FDA granted Breakthrough Therapy designation and priority review for Ianalumab (VAY736) for the treatment of Sjögren's disease following positive Phase III trial results demonstrating reduced disease activity.

The FDA approved Cosentyx (secukinumab) for the treatment of moderate to severe hidradenitis suppurativa in pediatric patients aged 12 years and older, making it the only IL-17A inhibitor approved for this population.
Novartis completed the acquisition of Excellergy to acquire Exl-111, a half-life extended, high-affinity anti-IgE monoclonal antibody in Phase I for allergic diseases.
Regulatory submissions for Cosentyx (secukinumab) for the treatment of polymyalgia rheumatica (PMR) were completed in the US, Europe, and Japan.

AbbVie submitted a regulatory application to the FDA for SKYRIZI (risankizumab-rzaa), a monoclonal antibody, seeking approval for subcutaneous induction in adults with moderately to severely active Crohn's disease. The submission is supported by positive data from the Phase 3 AFFIRM study, which evaluated the efficacy and safety of the subcutaneous formulation.

The US FDA approved the Saphnelo Pen, a new autoinjector for subcutaneous self-administration of anifrolumab, for treating adult patients with systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). This approval follows positive results from the Phase III TULIP-SC trial, offering patients a convenient once-weekly dosing option outside of clinical settings.

The US FDA approved the Saphnelo Pen, a new autoinjector for subcutaneous self-administration of anifrolumab, for treating adult patients with systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). This approval follows positive results from the Phase III TULIP-SC trial, offering patients a convenient once-weekly alternative to intravenous infusion.

gene editing.news: Astellas announced a strategic halt of AT132 (resamirigene bilparvovec) for X-linked myotubular myopathy and a redeployment of resources to ASP2957, a next-generation investigational gene therapy, resulting in a ¥16.4 billion impairment loss.

bispecific antibodies.news: WuXi Biologics announced the structural completion and arrival of key equipment at its Chengdu microbial commercial manufacturing site, designed to support the production of complex modalities including bispecific antibodies. The facility features a 15,000 L fermenter and will leverage the company's proprietary platforms like WuXiBody™ to manufacture these therapies at scale.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Chugai announced the approval of Lunsumio and Polivy combination therapy for an additional indication of relapsed or refractory large B-cell lymphoma. Additionally, Enspryng presented Phase III results in myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein antibody-associated disease (MOGAD) at the 2026 American Academy of Neurology Annual Meeting.

molecular glues.news: Proxygen is advancing first-in-class induced proximity therapeutics, specifically molecular glue degraders targeting p300 and CDK12 to reprogram transcriptional dependencies in cancer.

gene editing.news: The European Medicines Agency's CHMP adopted a positive opinion recommending marketing authorization for Itvisma (intrathecal onasemnogene abeparvovec), a one-time gene replacement therapy for spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) in patients aged two years and older, based on data from the STEER study.

bispecific antibodies.news: WuXi Biologics received GMP certification from the South Korea Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (MFDS) for three facilities, enabling commercial manufacturing of a bispecific antibody therapeutic for biliary tract cancer.

WuXi Biologics announced that three of its manufacturing facilities in Wuxi received GMP certification from the South Korea Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (MFDS). This certification enables the company to provide end-to-end commercial manufacturing services for a bispecific antibody indicated for the treatment of biliary tract cancer.

monoclonal antibodies.news: WuXi Biologics received South Korea MFDS GMP certification for three facilities, enabling commercial manufacturing of bispecific antibody therapeutics, a modality built upon monoclonal antibody platforms.

The US FDA approved Dupixent (dupilumab), a fully human monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of children aged two to 11 years with uncontrolled chronic spontaneous urticaria. This approval marks the first biologic medicine for this specific pediatric indication and is based on data from the LIBERTY-CUPID clinical study program.

The US FDA approved Dupixent (dupilumab), a monoclonal antibody, for treating children aged 2 to 11 with uncontrolled chronic spontaneous urticaria. This approval, based on the LIBERTY-CUPID clinical study program, marks the first biologic indication for this age group for this condition.

Merck's KEYTRUDA (pembrolizumab), a PD-1 inhibitor monoclonal antibody, received FDA approval for treating platinum-resistant ovarian cancer in combination with paclitaxel, with or without bevacizumab, based on Phase 3 KEYNOTE-B96 trial data showing improved progression-free and overall survival.
Roche announced positive Phase III results for Enspryng (satralizumab) in MOGAD, reducing relapse risk by 68%, marking a potential first treatment for the condition.
Roche reported positive Phase III results for Gazyva/Gazyvaro (obinutuzumab) in primary membranous nephropathy, achieving complete remission at two years.
The FDA accepted a New Drug Application for Gazyva/Gazyvaro for the treatment of systemic lupus erythematosus based on Phase III ALLEGORY data.

AbbVie announced a $1.4 billion investment to build a new manufacturing campus in Durham, North Carolina, specifically to support the production of its immunology and oncology medicines, which include monoclonal antibodies.

The US FDA approved Dupixent (dupilumab), a monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of children aged two to 11 years with uncontrolled chronic spontaneous urticaria (CSU). This approval marks the first biologic medicine for this specific pediatric indication and is based on data from the LIBERTY-CUPID clinical study program.

The US FDA approved Sanofi's Tzield (teplizumab), a CD3-directed monoclonal antibody, for an expanded indication to delay the onset of stage 3 type 1 diabetes in children aged one year and older with stage 2 T1D.

The US FDA extended the target action date for its review of the biologics license application for Sarclisa (isatuximab) subcutaneous formulation to July 23, 2026, for the treatment of multiple myeloma.

Disc Medicine announced it will present data from the RALLY-MF Phase 2 trial of DISC-0974, an anti-hemojuvelin monoclonal antibody, at the ASCO Annual Meeting for treating anemia in myelofibrosis patients.

The US FDA approved Sanofi's Tzield (teplizumab-mzwv), a CD3-directed monoclonal antibody, for an expanded indication to delay the onset of stage 3 type 1 diabetes in children aged one year and older with stage 2 disease.

The FDA extended the target action date for its review of Sanofi's Sarclisa (isatuximab-irfc) subcutaneous formulation for multiple myeloma to July 23, 2026, while the EMA's CHMP adopted a positive opinion for the same subcutaneous version.

Genentech announced that its Phase III METEOROID study met its primary endpoint, showing Enspryng (satralizumab) reduced the risk of relapses by 68% in patients with myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein antibody-associated disease (MOGAD). The company plans to submit these data to regulatory authorities globally to seek approval for the first treatment for this rare autoimmune disease.

Pfizer presented updated Phase 2 data for PF-08634404 (PF'4404), a novel bispecific antibody targeting PD-1 and VEGF, as monotherapy in first-line PD-L1-expressing non-small cell lung cancer. The company also highlighted ongoing Phase 3 studies evaluating this bispecific antibody in combination with chemotherapy for NSCLC and colorectal cancer.

Roche announced that its humanized monoclonal antibody ENSPRYNG (satralizumab) met the primary endpoint in the Phase III METEOROID study for MOGAD, reducing relapse risk by 68% compared to placebo. Data will be submitted to regulatory authorities globally for potential approval as the first treatment for this rare autoimmune disease.
Merck & Co. and Eisai provided an update on the Phase 3 LITESPARK-012 trial evaluating first-line combination treatments for advanced renal cell carcinoma.

Chugai announced new data from the METEOROID study of Enspryng (satralizumab) for myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein antibody-associated disease (MOGAD), presented at the 2026 American Academy of Neurology Annual Meeting.
Disc Medicine announced it will present data from the RALLY-MF Phase 2 trial of DISC-0974, an anti-hemojuvelin antibody, for anemia in myelofibrosis at the ASCO Annual Meeting.

bispecific antibodies.news: Pfizer presented updated Phase 2 data for PF-08634404 (SSGJ-707), a novel bispecific antibody targeting PD-1 and VEGF, as monotherapy in first-line PD-L1-expressing non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) at ASCO 2026. The company also highlighted ongoing Phase 3 studies (Symbiotic-Lung-01 and Symbiotic-GI-03) evaluating the agent in combination with chemotherapy for NSCLC and colorectal cancer.

monoclonal antibodies.news: The FDA accepted Genentech's supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for Gazyva (obinutuzumab), a monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). The filing is based on positive Phase III ALLEGORY study data showing significant disease activity reduction compared to placebo, with a PDUFA decision expected by December 2026.

The FDA accepted Genentech's supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for Gazyva (obinutuzumab), a humanized monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE). The filing is based on positive Phase III ALLEGORY study data showing significant disease activity reduction compared to placebo, with an approval decision expected by December 2026.

The U.S. FDA granted Priority Review to a supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for the combination of PADCEV (enfortumab vedotin) and Keytruda (pembrolizumab) for perioperative treatment of muscle-invasive bladder cancer regardless of cisplatin eligibility, based on Phase 3 EV-304 trial data.

The U.S. FDA accepted a supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for Gazyva/Gazyvaro (obinutuzumab) for the treatment of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE), based on positive Phase 3 ALLEGORY study results showing significant disease activity reduction.

A prespecified interim analysis of the Phase 3 I CAN trial demonstrated that Ultomiris (ravulizumab) met its primary endpoint, showing a statistically significant and clinically meaningful reduction in proteinuria in adults with immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN).

AstraZeneca announced that tozorakimab, a first-in-class IL-33-targeting monoclonal antibody, met its primary endpoint in the Phase III MIRANDA trial for COPD. The drug demonstrated a statistically significant reduction in moderate-to-severe exacerbations in former smokers and the overall population.

Kyowa Kirin announced the discontinuation of all ongoing clinical trials for rocatinlimab, an investigational anti-OX40 monoclonal antibody, due to emerging safety concerns regarding malignancies, including cases of Kaposi's sarcoma.

Kyowa Kirin terminated its collaboration with Amgen to regain full control of the global development and commercialization program for rocatinlimab, an anti-OX40 monoclonal antibody for atopic dermatitis, following a strategic portfolio prioritization by Amgen.

GSK announced that the National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) of China approved Blenrep (belantamab mafodotin), a monoclonal antibody-drug conjugate, in combination with bortezomib and dexamethasone for the treatment of adults with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma who have received at least one prior line of therapy.

AstraZeneca reported that tozorakimab, a first-in-class monoclonal antibody targeting IL-33, met its primary endpoint in the Phase III MIRANDA trial, demonstrating a statistically significant reduction in the annualized rate of moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations in former smokers and the overall population.

Immunocore presented five-year overall survival data for KIMMTRAK (tebentafusp-tebn), a bispecific TCR-CD3 protein, at the AACR 2026 meeting, showing it doubled the likelihood of being alive at five years compared to the control arm in first-line HLA-A*02:01-positive patients with metastatic uveal melanoma.

Kyowa Kirin announced the discontinuation of all ongoing clinical trials for rocatinlimab, an investigational anti-OX40 monoclonal antibody, due to emerging safety concerns regarding malignancies including Kaposi's sarcoma.
Kyowa Kirin terminated its collaboration with Amgen to regain full global control of the rocatinlimab development and commercialization program for atopic dermatitis.

GSK announced that the National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) of China has approved Blenrep (belantamab mafodotin), an antibody-drug conjugate targeting BCMA, for the treatment of relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma in combination with bortezomib and dexamethasone.

AstraZeneca reported positive results from the Phase III MIRANDA trial for tozorakimab, an IL-33-targeting monoclonal antibody, which met its primary endpoint by significantly reducing the annualized rate of moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations.

bispecific antibodies.news: Immunocore presented five-year overall survival data for KIMMTRAK (tebentafusp), a TCR-bispecific protein, at the AACR 2026 meeting. The data showed that KIMMTRAK doubled the likelihood of being alive at five years compared to the control arm in patients with metastatic uveal melanoma.

Immunocore presented five-year overall survival data for KIMMTRAK (tebentafusp), a TCR-bispecific protein, at the AACR 2026 meeting, showing it doubled the likelihood of survival at five years compared to the control arm in patients with metastatic uveal melanoma.

Gilead Sciences received all required regulatory approvals to proceed with its acquisition of Arcellx, a company specializing in cell therapies and bispecific antibodies. The company extended its tender offer for Arcellx shares to $115.00 per share plus contingent value rights, pending the expiration of a 14-day waiting period from Australian competition authorities.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Gilead Sciences received all required regulatory approvals to proceed with its acquisition of Arcellx, a company developing bispecific antibodies and CAR-T therapies, and extended the tender offer deadline to April 27, 2026.

gene editing.news: Gilead Sciences received all required regulatory approvals to proceed with its acquisition of Arcellx, a company developing anito-cel, a CAR-T cell therapy for multiple myeloma. The tender offer was extended to April 27, 2026, with a purchase price of $115.00 per share plus contingent value rights tied to future sales milestones.

UCB announced a definitive agreement to acquire Neurona Therapeutics for up to $1.15 billion to advance regenerative cell therapies for epilepsy. The deal includes NRTX-1001, a neuronal cell therapy candidate currently in Phase I/II trials, which utilizes pluripotent stem cell technology to restore neural circuitry.

bispecific antibodies.news: Hinge Bio announced the first subject dosed in a Phase 1 clinical trial of HB2198, a novel multispecific antibody candidate targeting CD19 and CD20 for treating autoimmune diseases. The company has initiated two Phase 1 trials: one in healthy volunteers to assess safety and tolerability, and another in patients with Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE).

monoclonal antibodies.news: GSK completed the acquisition of 35Pharma Inc. for $950 million, gaining access to HS235, a novel protein-based therapeutic targeting the activin receptor signaling pathway for pulmonary hypertension. While the press release describes HS235 as a protein-based therapeutic, the acquisition expands GSK's respiratory and immunology pipeline with a differentiated mechanism of action.

Hinge Bio announced the first subject dosed in a Phase 1 clinical trial of HB2198, a novel multispecific antibody candidate targeting CD19 and CD20 for treating autoimmune diseases. The company has initiated two Phase 1 trials, one in healthy volunteers and another in patients with Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE).

molecular glues.news: STORM Therapeutics secured $56 million in Series C financing and dosed the first patient in a Phase 2 sarcoma trial for STC-15, a first-in-class METTL3 inhibitor that functions as a molecular glue to reprogram malignant progenitor cells.

monoclonal antibodies.news: BigHat Biosciences highlights its in-house cell-free protein synthesis (CFPS) manufacturing capabilities, which enable the overnight expression and rapid characterization of thousands of antibodies using its Milliner platform.

The European Commission approved Dupixent (dupilumab), a monoclonal antibody, for treating moderate-to-severe chronic spontaneous urticaria in children aged two to 11 years, based on data from the LIBERTY-CUPID clinical study program.

The European Commission approved Dupixent (dupilumab), a monoclonal antibody, as the first targeted medicine to treat children aged 2 to 11 with chronic spontaneous urticaria. This approval expands the drug's indication in the EU based on data from the LIBERTY-CUPID clinical study program.

GSK presented positive phase I data for mocertatug rezetecan, an antibody-drug conjugate featuring a fully human anti-B7-H4 monoclonal antibody, showing high response rates in ovarian and endometrial cancers. These results support the initiation of five pivotal phase III trials in 2026.

AbbVie showcased late-breaking phase 2 data for mirvetuximab soravtansine-gynx (ELAHERE), an antibody-drug conjugate containing a folate receptor alpha-binding antibody, demonstrating a 62.7% objective response rate in platinum-sensitive ovarian cancer. The data supports further investigation of the drug in combination with carboplatin.

bispecific antibodies.news: Alexion presented new data from the PREVAIL Phase III trial evaluating gefurulimab, a novel dual-binding nanobody, for the treatment of adults with anti-acetylcholine receptor antibody-positive generalized myasthenia gravis at the 2026 AAN Annual Meeting.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Alexion presented new data at the 2026 AAN Annual Meeting from the PREVAIL Phase III trial, showing statistically significant improvements with the investigational C5 inhibitor gefurulimab for adults with generalized myasthenia gravis. The company also shared results on ravulizumab (Ultomiris) in neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder and selumetinib (Koselugo) for neurofibromatosis type 1.

gene editing.news: Cartherics and Catalent expanded their commercial license agreement to utilize a Catalent cGMP-compliant induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC) line for manufacturing CAR-NK cell therapies. The partnership leverages Catalent's validated workflows for gene editing to support the development of Cartherics' lead product, CTH-401, for ovarian cancer and endometriosis.

Généthon scientists published a study in Molecular Therapy detailing a CRISPR/Cas9 strategy to increase utrophin expression in Duchenne muscular dystrophy by disabling a microRNA binding site. The approach demonstrated improved muscle function and reduced fibrosis in preclinical models, offering a potential universal therapy independent of specific DMD mutations.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Chugai Pharmaceutical and Zenyaku Kogyo announced the termination of their co-promotion agreement for Rituxan (rituximab), an anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody, in Japan effective September 3, 2026. Following the transition, Zenyaku Kogyo will assume sole responsibility for sales and medical information activities for the product.

GSK announced that China's NMPA approved Exdensur (depemokimab), an ultra-long-acting IL-5 monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps (CRSwNP). The approval is based on data from the ANCHOR-1 and ANCHOR-2 phase III trials.

GSK announced that China's National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) has approved Exdensur (depemokimab), an ultra-long-acting anti-IL-5 monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps (CRSwNP). The approval is based on data from the ANCHOR-1 and ANCHOR-2 phase III trials showing significant improvements in nasal polyp size and obstruction.

Gilead Sciences entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Tubulis GmbH for $3.15 billion in upfront cash and up to $1.85 billion in milestone payments. The acquisition adds Tubulis' next-generation antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) platform and clinical-stage assets, including TUB-040 and TUB-030, to Gilead's oncology pipeline.

Evotec secured a multi-year BioMaP-Consortium award from BARDA valued at up to $10 million to optimize the biomanufacturing of monoclonal antibodies against Ebola and Sudan viruses using its continuous manufacturing platform.

Evotec received a new Gates Foundation grant to advance global access to affordable biotherapeutics by applying its AI-driven J.MD molecular design platform to improve monoclonal antibody developability for high-priority global health indications.

molecular glues.news: Evotec's CELMoD molecular glue candidate BMS-986506 progressed from IND acceptance in November 2025 into a Phase 1 clinical study in March 2026 for renal cell carcinoma, in collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb.

gene editing.news: CRISPR Therapeutics announced that senior management will present at the 25th Annual Needham Virtual Healthcare Conference on April 13, 2026. The company highlighted its leadership in gene editing, including the approved CASGEVY therapy and its proprietary SyNTase editing platform.

bispecific antibodies.news: Sanofi announced that its bispecific Nanobody lunsekimig met primary and key secondary endpoints in Phase 2 studies for moderate-to-severe asthma (AIRCULES) and chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps (DUET). However, the drug failed to meet its primary endpoint in an exploratory Phase 2 study for atopic dermatitis (VELVET).

Immunocore announced that its management team will present at the 25th Annual Needham Virtual Healthcare Conference to discuss its pipeline of TCR bispecific immunotherapies (ImmTAX), including its approved product KIMMTRAK for uveal melanoma.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Sanofi announced that lunsekimig, a novel bispecific Nanobody (VHH) targeting TSLP and IL-13, met primary and key secondary endpoints in Phase 2 studies for asthma and chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps (CRSwNP), though it failed to meet the primary endpoint in atopic dermatitis.

Amgen reported positive topline results from a Phase 3 trial for subcutaneous TEPEZZA (teprotumumab), a monoclonal antibody for Thyroid Eye Disease, which met its primary endpoint with a 77% proptosis response rate and demonstrated efficacy comparable to the intravenous formulation.

gene editing.news: Affinia Therapeutics received approval from Health Canada to initiate the UPBEAT trial, a Phase 1/2 clinical study investigating AFTX-201, an AAV gene therapy for BAG3-associated dilated cardiomyopathy. This follows FDA acceptance of the IND and Fast Track designation for the investigational medicine.

Affinia Therapeutics received approval from Health Canada to initiate the UPBEAT trial, a Phase 1/2 clinical study investigating AFTX-201, an AAV gene therapy for BAG3-associated dilated cardiomyopathy. This follows FDA acceptance of the IND application and Fast Track designation for the investigational medicine.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Lipocine reported topline safety and efficacy results for LPCN 1154 (oral brexanolone) in a Phase 3 trial for postpartum depression. While the primary endpoint was not met in the overall population, a post hoc analysis in patients with a history of psychiatric conditions showed statistically significant and clinically meaningful reductions in HAM-D scores. The drug demonstrated a favorable safety profile with no severe adverse events.

gene editing.news: Caris Life Sciences launched Caris ChromoSeq, the world's first Whole Genome and Whole Transcriptome Tumor Profiling assay specifically designed for myeloid malignancies. The test aims to replace fragmented diagnostic workflows with a single, integrated solution for acute myeloid leukemia, myelodysplastic syndromes, and myeloproliferative neoplasms.

bispecific antibodies.news: Positive results from the EMERALD-3 Phase III trial showed that Imfinzi (durvalumab) plus Imjudo (tremelimumab), combined with lenvatinib and TACE, significantly improved progression-free survival in patients with unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma eligible for embolization.
